title: Sweet Dreams Are Made of Cheese(cake)
story: Bakeries across America pile their display cases high with creamy, dreamy cheesecakes every July 30 for National Cheesecake Day! Restaurants offer special deals on classic New York style, chocolate swirl, and berry-topped varieties. Home bakers whip up their own versions using cream cheese, eggs, and graham cracker crusts. Social media fills with drool-worthy photos of perfect slices. Some people host cheesecake parties where everyone brings a different flavor to share. activity_question: What do bakeries typically do on National Cheesecake Day?
activity_choices: Hide all their cheesecakes in secret locations
Pile their display cases high with cheesecakes
Close early to eat cheesecake themselves
activity_answer: Pile their display cases high with cheesecakes
bonus_fact: New York cheesecake uses cream cheese, while Italian cheesecake traditionally uses ricotta cheese.

title: Pocket-Sized Reading Revolution Day
story: Every July 30, book lovers celebrate National Paperback Book Day by diving into their favorite soft-cover stories! Paperback books changed everything because they're cheaper, lighter, and fit perfectly in backpacks and purses. Today, people share photos of their paperback collections on social media, visit bookstores for special sales, and start new adventures between those bendable covers. Libraries often host reading marathons where kids can trade paperbacks with friends. The best part? You can take these books anywhere without worrying about damaging expensive hardcovers!

They're always written by famous authors

activity_answer: They're cheaper and lighter than hardcover books
bonus_fact: The first paperback books were sold for just 25 cents in 1935!

title: Farewell to a Bug-Shaped Icon
story: Beep beep! The beloved Volkswagen Beetle said goodbye on July 30, 2003. After decades of bringing smiles to drivers, the last 'old style' Beetle rolled off the assembly line in Mexico. This quirky car, shaped like a round bug, had been a favorite for generations. Its friendly face and compact size made it perfect for zipping through city streets.
activity_question: What shape was the Volkswagen Beetle compared to?
bonus_fact: The original Beetle design was created in the 1930s and barely changed for over 60 years!

title: Henry Moore: Shaping Art History
story: A giant of modern sculpture was born on July 30, 1898. Henry Moore, the English artist who would change how we see shapes, entered the world in Castleford. His famous work, Three Piece Sculpture: Vertebrae, shows how he turned bones and nature into amazing art. Moore's big, curvy sculptures became world-famous, inspiring people to look at art in new ways.
activity_question: What famous work by Henry Moore is mentioned in the story?
follow_up_question: How do you think Moore's sculptures might have been inspired by bones and nature?

title: A Star is Born in San Francisco
story: Victor Wong burst onto the scene in San Francisco on July 30, 1927. This talented actor would later dazzle audiences in movies like 'Big Trouble in Little China'. Wong's journey from the City by the Bay to the big screen showed that with passion and hard work, anyone can become a star.
activity_question: In which movie did Victor Wong become famous for his role?
follow_up_question: What other talents might an actor need besides acting?

title: Towering Talent: Bill Cartwright's Basketball Journey
story: Bill Cartwright, a giant on the basketball court, entered the world in 1957. Born in Lodi, California on July 30, he grew up to become a formidable center. Cartwright's skills shone bright as he earned a spot in the 1980 NBA All-Star game while playing for the New York Knicks. Later, he helped the Chicago Bulls clinch three consecutive NBA Championships from 1991 to 1993.
activity_question: Which team did Bill Cartwright help win three consecutive NBA Championships?
follow_up_question: What position did Bill Cartwright play in basketball?

title: Bluegrass Star Ron Block Born
story: Banjos twanged and guitars strummed as Ron Block entered the world in Gardena, California. Born on July 30, 1964, this future bluegrass sensation would go on to win Grammy Awards with the band Alison Krauss & Union Station. Block's nimble fingers on banjo and guitar, along with his soulful singing and songwriting, have made him a beloved figure in the world of bluegrass music.
activity_question: What instruments does Ron Block play?
follow_up_question: Have you ever heard bluegrass music? What do you like about it?

title: From Football Field to TV Star
story: Terry Crews tackled life head-on! Born in Flint, Michigan in 1968, he first made his mark as an NFL linebacker for the Washington Redskins. But Crews didn't stop there. He flexed his acting muscles and became a beloved star on the hit TV show Brooklyn Nine-Nine. His journey from July 30th football player to comedy icon shows that with hard work, you can score big in any field!
activity_question: What TV show did Terry Crews become a star on?
follow_up_question: What sport did Terry Crews play professionally before becoming an actor?

title: A Rising Star is Born
story: A future Hollywood sensation entered the world on July 30, 1999. Joey King, destined to become a famous American actress, was born in the heart of the entertainment industry - Los Angeles, California. Little did anyone know that this newborn would grow up to captivate audiences in popular films and TV shows.
activity_question: Where was Joey King born?
follow_up_question: Can you name a movie or TV show Joey King has acted in?
